name: The World of Solo Leveling
description: A comprehensive lorebook covering the original Solo Leveling story (web novel/manhwa/2024 anime), from Sung Jinwoo's first dungeon through the Monarchs War and the true ending, including characters, locations, factions, races, the System/power hierarchy, and major story arcs. Excludes the Solo Leveling: Ragnarok sequel.

1.content: Solo Leveling takes place on a modern-day Earth where dimensional Gates began appearing decades ago, each connecting to a dungeon full of monsters and treasure. Licensed Hunters, ranked E-Rank to S-Rank by combat power, clear Gates and fight the monsters that emerge. Most Hunters awaken fixed magical abilities, but Sung Jinwoo alone is chosen as a Player by a mysterious System that lets him level up indefinitely, gain stats and skills through quests, and command an army of Shadow Soldiers raised from slain enemies' corpses. Beyond Earth lie Outer Universes home to ancient cosmic forces: the Rulers, servants of a slain god called the Absolute Being, and the Monarchs, rulers of monstrous armies who have warred against the Rulers since the dawn of time. Earth's Gates are side effects of this war, as Monarchs and Rulers seek human vessels to fight through. The System was secretly built to prepare Sung Jinwoo as the vessel of Ashborn, the Shadow Monarch, and after Ashborn's betrayal of the other Monarchs and death, Sung Jinwoo inherits his power and becomes the second Shadow Monarch, defeating the remaining Monarchs and the Rulers' enemies to become the strongest being on Earth.
1.name: The World of Solo Leveling

2.content: The Ahjin Guild is a Korean guild founded and led by Sung Jinwoo, who serves as its Guild Master and only S-Rank hunter. Yoo Jinho, a D-Rank tanker, serves as Vice-Guild Master, and Yoo Soohyun, an A-Rank hunter, rounds out the guild's three official human members. Despite its tiny official roster, the Ahjin Guild commands Sung Jinwoo's army of shadow soldiers, an force of resurrected monsters and slain enemies that grows with every battle Sung Jinwoo fights. When the shadow army is counted, the Ahjin Guild surpasses every other guild in the world in both raw strength and numbers, making it the most powerful guild in existence despite having the smallest membership of any top-tier guild. The Ahjin Guild operates independently of the larger five-guild hierarchy that dominates Korean hunter politics, with Sung Jinwoo answering to no other guild master.
2.name: Ahjin Guild

3.content: Antares was the King of Dragons, the Monarch of Destruction, and the strongest and oldest of the nine Monarchs. Antares was a tall, muscular man with long red hair, a red beard, glowing red eyes, and reddish-silver armor, and could transform into an enormous red dragon or a human-dragon hybrid through Spiritual Body Manifestation. Antares reveled in destruction and held humanity in contempt, but he possessed a sense of honor, was willing to negotiate with worthy opponents, and cared for his own minions. Antares wielded immeasurable strength and speed, near-total invulnerability, Breath of Destruction, and Dragon's Fear, and was considered even stronger than the Shadow Monarch. After Sung Jinwoo killed the other Monarchs, Antares invaded Earth through Canada, massacred millions of people, and fought Sung Jinwoo in a battle that ended with the Rulers descending and killing Antares. Antares was revived by the Cup of Reincarnation and, twenty-seven years later, was hunted down and killed by Sung Jinwoo alone, marking the end of the Monarchs as a threat to Earth. Antares was later revived again through a bargain with Sung Jinwoo to help groom Sung Suho as the next Monarch of Destruction.
3.name: Antares

4.content: Kandiaru, also known as the Architect, was the hidden boss of the Double Dungeon beneath Cartenon Temple and the moderator of the System. Kandiaru took the form of a large stone statue of a hooded angel with six wings, eight arms, glowing red eyes, and black sclera, hiding a face of whitish-blue skin and light blue eyes beneath a mask. Kandiaru was psychopathic, egotistical, and fanatically loyal to the Monarchs, whom he worshiped as gods. Kandiaru created the System at Ashborn's request to help find a human vessel for the Shadow Monarch, and he tested Sung Jinwoo with an army of stone puppets before revealing himself as the final test. Kandiaru possessed immense strength, speed, and durability, near-total control over the System, and the ability to command his statues like puppets, but Sung Jinwoo defeated him after Ashborn secretly betrayed Kandiaru and locked him out of the System, allowing Sung Jinwoo to break free and destroy Kandiaru's body. In the timeline reshaped by the Cup of Reincarnation, Kandiaru survived as the new Monarch of the Underworld, fled beyond the known universe, betrayed his home reality to an Outer God called an Itarim, and triggered the Outer God War.
4.name: Architect

5.content: Ashborn was the King of the Dead, the Monarch of Shadows, and the first Shadow Monarch, as well as the Greatest Fragment of Brilliant Light and the strongest of the Rulers. Ashborn appeared as a large shadow warrior with glowing purple hair and eyes, curved black horns, and jet-black armor, though as a Ruler he took the form of a white-winged knight with silver hair. Ashborn cared deeply about life and desired peace between the Rulers and the Monarchs, having been the only Ruler who stayed loyal to the Absolute Being. Left for dead after that rebellion, Ashborn discovered a hidden monstrous power inside himself and transformed into the Shadow Monarch, later fighting alongside the Monarchs against the Rulers. Ashborn commanded absolute control over shadows and darkness, possessed immeasurable strength, and could bestow his powers on a worthy human vessel. Ashborn spent years guiding Sung Jinwoo's growth through the System, ultimately betraying his ally Kandiaru and giving Sung Jinwoo his black heart so that Sung Jinwoo could become his successor as the second Shadow Monarch. Having passed on his power and his unfinished war, Ashborn found the peace he had long sought.
5.name: Ashborn

6.content: Beru is a shadow soldier in Sung Jinwoo's army, created when Sung Jinwoo extracted the shadow of the Ant King after killing it on Jeju Island. Beru appears as a huge humanoid shadow ant with a light purple glow, neon eyes, sharp claws, and smoky wings, though after later being given to Sung Suho he loses most of his power and is reduced to a floating head. Beru is bloodthirsty and brutal in combat, holding the rank of Marshal and ranking just below Igris in strength among Sung Jinwoo's shadows, but outside of battle he is fiercely loyal and has an oddly old-fashioned manner of speech from watching Korean period dramas. Beru possesses immense strength and speed, regeneration, healing magic, flight, paralysis poison, and the ability to absorb the skills of those he consumes. Beru sparred with Cha Hae-In during her guild entry test and nearly killed her before Sung Jinwoo intervened, and he later fought alongside Sung Jinwoo against the Monarchs. Beru grows deeply attached to Sung Jinwoo's son, Sung Suho, and is devastated when Sung Suho must forget him, but Beru later helps Sirka against the dragon Nidhogg near the World Tree, gaining vast new power from the Apostle of Nightmares.
6.name: Beru

7.content: Cartenon Temple is a hidden S-Rank dungeon, also called the Double Dungeon, that appears nested inside ordinary D-Rank dungeons. It served as the base of the Architect, Kandiaru, who used it to find a human host worthy of inheriting the Shadow Monarch Ashborn's power. Instead of monsters, the temple is filled with giant statues that enforce three Commandments inscribed on a stone epitaph: worship the Statue of God by bowing, play the temple's instrument-statues in unison to raise a central altar, and have one person remain on that altar while the doors seal and the other statues advance. Sung Jinwoo first entered Cartenon Temple during a raid that killed eleven of seventeen hunters, sacrificed himself to fulfill the third commandment, and was offered and accepted the role of Player of the System, which let him survive. When Jinwoo later reached level 100, Kandiaru summoned him back, had him defeat the Statue of God and other puppets, then fought him directly as Ashborn's final test. Jinwoo destroyed Kandiaru after Ashborn took control of Jinwoo's body and locked Kandiaru out of the System, while Woo Jinchul, Choi Jong-In, and Cha Hae-In fought off Kandiaru's statues alongside him.
7.name: Cartenon Temple

8.content: Cha Hae-In is a Korean S-Rank hunter and the Vice-Guild Master of the Hunters Guild. Cha Hae-In is a slender, blonde-haired woman whose eyes glow yellow when she uses her abilities, and she favors red-themed armor and a black sword with a silver hilt. Cha Hae-In is serious, meticulous, and humble toward lower-ranked hunters, though she is socially awkward outside of dungeon raiding. Cha Hae-In has an unusual mana sensitivity that makes most hunters and monsters smell foul to her, but Sung Jinwoo is the only person whose scent she finds pleasant, which first draws her to him. Cha Hae-In nearly died fighting the Ant King on Jeju Island before Sung Jinwoo saved her, and she later tried to join his guild, eventually confessing her feelings for him. Cha Hae-In fought through the Monarchs War and the battle against Antares, and after the Rulers used the Cup of Reincarnation to reset the timeline, she lost her memories of being a hunter but reunited with Sung Jinwoo, married him, and gave birth to their son, Sung Suho, a pregnancy that nearly killed her due to his immense latent power.
8.name: Cha Hae-In

9.content: The Double Dungeon was the first dungeon Sung Jinwoo entered as an E-Rank hunter, a low-level dungeon that concealed a second, far deadlier dungeon beneath it. Inside the hidden temple below, Sung Jinwoo's party was slaughtered by stone statue guardians, and Sung Jinwoo alone survived by praying before a mysterious altar. The altar belonged to a being called the System, which offered Sung Jinwoo a chance to become a Player in exchange for completing deadly trials, and Sung Jinwoo accepted rather than die. Sung Jinwoo nearly died again completing the System's trials but survived and emerged with the Player ability, gaining the power to level up like a game character. Later, Sung Jinwoo returned to the temple seeking answers about the System and discovered an ancient magical beast disguised as the Statue of God, who had been secretly behind the original massacre. Sung Jinwoo defeated this beast in single combat, killing it and absorbing its Black Heart, and during the fight his skill Dominator's Touch evolved into its final form. The System also revealed to Sung Jinwoo the existence of an ancient conflict that would later reach Earth as the war between the Rulers and the Monarchs.
9.name: Double Dungeon

10.content: Gates are blue portals that began appearing worldwide about ten years before Sung Jinwoo's story began, opened by the Rulers to saturate the human world with mana before their war against the Monarchs. Each Gate connects to a Dungeon, a pocket dimension full of magic beasts led by a boss, and stays open until that boss dies. A Dungeon left uncleared for seven days suffers a Dungeon Break, releasing its monsters into the human world. Gates and Dungeons are ranked E through S, with higher ranks emitting more mana and growing larger, up to hurricane-sized S-Rank Gates. A Red Gate is a rare, lethal variant that can form from a B-Rank or higher Gate: once enough people enter, it seals shut, stops emitting mana, transports victims into a hostile environment, and runs time faster than the outside world. Escape requires killing the boss or surviving until a Dungeon Break.
10.name: Gates and Dungeons

11.content: Go Gunhee was a Korean S-Rank hunter and the Chairman of the Korean Hunters Association. Go Gunhee was a muscular old man with gray hair and numerous scars, whose eyes glowed yellow when he used his powers, and he was secretly the human vessel of one of the Rulers. Go Gunhee was humble, honest, and a workaholic who deeply respected Sung Jinwoo's resolve to support his family despite the danger, and he used his authority to grant Sung Jinwoo permission to raid high-rank gates alone. Go Gunhee possessed immense strength and speed, Ruler's Authority, and Spiritual Body Manifestation, though his old age limited how long he could fight. Go Gunhee was fatally stabbed through the chest by the Monarch Sillad while buying time for Sung Jinwoo to arrive, and he died asking Sung Jinwoo to remain on the side of humanity in the coming war with the Monarchs. After the Cup of Reincarnation reset the timeline, Go Gunhee never became a hunter, but Sung Jinwoo secretly visited him in old age, gave him the Holy Water of Life to extend his life by ten years, and eventually restored his memories of their friendship before Go Gunhee died peacefully.
11.name: Go Gunhee

12.content: Hunter Ranks are the international E-through-S system for measuring the strength of anyone who radiates mana, including hunters, gates, and magic beasts. E-Rank is the weakest and S-Rank the strongest, with a large power gap between each rank. A hunter's rank is fixed at awakening and almost never changes, since a rank-raising second awakening is extremely rare. Rank determines income and status: E-Rank hunters are paid little and treated as disposable, while S-Rank hunters live like royalty. Fewer than one in five thousand awakened individuals rank above D. False Rankers are hunters who consciously suppress their mana output to register below their true strength, and many are dangerous killers hiding among lower ranks.
12.name: Hunter Ranks

13.content: The Hunters Guild is the largest and strongest guild among the top five guilds in Korea, operating out of the Seoul Metropolitan Area. Choi Jong-In serves as Guild Master, and Cha Hae-In serves as Vice-Guild Master, making the Hunters Guild the only Korean guild with two S-Rank hunters on its roster. Because of its size, the Hunters Guild fields two strike squads instead of one. Strike Squad A is led jointly by Choi Jong-In and Cha Hae-In and consists entirely of A-Rank hunters, while Strike Squad B is led by Son Kihoon and mixes A-Rank and B-Rank hunters, including Gina, Lee Bora, Han Semi, and Seo Jiwoo. The Hunters Guild also maintains a Mining Team led by Bae Yoon-Suk that extracts magic ore from cleared dungeons. The Hunters Guild serves as the official protector of the Korean Hunters Auction. Strike Squad B repeatedly works alongside Sung Jinwoo on dangerous raids, including the reconnaissance mission to Jeju Island.
13.name: Hunters Guild

14.content: Igris is a shadow soldier in Sung Jinwoo's army, originally the Blood-Red Commander and boss of the dungeon where Sung Jinwoo underwent his job change. Igris appears as a tall, slender knight in blood-red armor with a horned helmet, glowing white eyes, a tattered red cape, and a massive bronze sword. Igris is defined by his loyalty and chivalry, refusing to serve Sung Jinwoo as a shadow soldier until given a genuine reason to follow him, and he discarded his sword to fight Sung Jinwoo bare-handed once the latter chose to do the same. Igris nearly defeated Sung Jinwoo in their original battle through superior strength, speed, and swordsmanship, and was only beaten when Sung Jinwoo drove a dagger into his eye and finished him with repeated stabs. It was later revealed that Igris had originally served Ashborn as one of his lieutenants and had been sent to test Sung Jinwoo's worthiness as Ashborn's chosen successor. As one of Sung Jinwoo's strongest shadows, Igris later sparred with Cha Hae-In during her guild entry test, wielding Baran's sword, and lost to her.
14.name: Igris

15.content: Jeju Island is a Korean island in the Pacific Ocean between South Korea and Japan. An S-Rank Gate spawned on Jeju Island and broke into a dungeon break, releasing a hive of mutant ants led by the Ant Queen and turning the island into an uninhabitable wasteland with thousands of civilian deaths. The Korean Hunters Association failed three search-and-destroy missions over the following years, losing the S-Rank Hunter Eunseok on the third attempt, and the Korean government eventually abandoned the island. After the ants evolved wings and began attacking nearby islands, Korea's S-Rank Hunters joined Japan's Draw Sword Guild for a fourth raid. The Ant King, the Ant Queen's strongest offspring, ambushed the raid and killed eight of the sixteen S-Rank Hunters before Sung Jinwoo arrived alone and killed the Ant King in a prolonged battle. Jinwoo then spent two days clearing the remaining ants and extracted the Ant King's corpse as the Shadow Soldier Beru. The raid's success made Jinwoo a national hero in Korea and a major figure in international politics.
15.name: Jeju Island

16.content: The Korean Hunters Association is the governmental body that oversees all hunters, gates, and guilds within Korea, headquartered in the Guro district of Seoul. Go Gunhee served as Chairman of the Korean Hunters Association and was one of the seven human vessels chosen by the Rulers, ranking as the second strongest hunter in Korea until his death. Woo Jinchul, an A-Rank fighter who previously led the Association's Surveillance Department, succeeded Go Gunhee as Chairman. The Surveillance Department also includes Kang Taeshik, a B-Rank assassin, and Han Jaehyuk, a C-Rank tanker, while Jung Gi-Soo works in the Evaluation Office. The Korean Hunters Association coordinates national responses to S-Rank gates and dungeon breaks, and its leadership crosses paths repeatedly with Sung Jinwoo and the Ahjin Guild during national crises. Go Gunhee was assassinated by the Monarch Sillad during the International Guild Conference, prompting Woo Jinchul's rise to Chairman.
16.name: Korean Hunters Association

17.content: The Monarchs are an ancient race of nine monster rulers created when the Absolute Being split the darkness at the dawn of time, born to destroy the world and locked in an eternal war against their counterparts, the Rulers. After the Absolute Being was murdered by the Rulers, the Rulers captured one Monarch alive and nearly won the war, until the Shadow Monarch Ashborn allied with the surviving Monarchs to restore the balance of power. Some Monarchs later betrayed Ashborn, who shattered his own shadow army and went into hiding while the Rulers defeated the remaining Monarchs and drove them into the cracks between worlds. Centuries later, several Monarchs invaded Seoul and then launched a final assault on the human world to stop Sung Jinwoo, the second Shadow Monarch, but Sung Jinwoo and the Rulers destroyed them all, ending the Monarchs War. After the Rulers undid the destruction, Sung Jinwoo spent twenty-seven years hunting down every remaining Monarch, leaving himself the last Monarch alive. The other eight Monarch titles have since passed to new successors.
17.name: Monarchs

18.comment: Monarchs War and the Final Battle
18.content: The Monarchs War began when the Monarchs, ancient rulers of the magical beasts, resumed their conflict with the Rulers by hunting down the human vessels the Rulers had chosen on Earth, including Go Gunhee, chairman of the Korean Hunters Association, killed by the Monarch Sillad. Sung Jinwoo became a target himself and was killed in battle against the Monarchs Rakan and Sillad, but his death triggered a hidden passive skill: the System removed all of his power limits, his shadow soldiers were restored to full strength, and the previous Shadow Monarch, Ashborn, returned to the World of Eternal Slumber after passing his full power to Sung Jinwoo. Sung Jinwoo revived, killed both Rakan and Sillad, and Ashborn's shadow army swore loyalty to him as their new master. In the Final Battle that followed, the strongest Monarch, Antares the King of Dragons, emerged from a gate in Canada and slaughtered hunters before Sung Jinwoo and his allies engaged him. Before Sung Jinwoo could land the killing blow, the Rulers arrived, finished off Antares themselves, and rewound time by ten years, undoing the war's devastation and resetting the world to before the System appeared.
18.name: Monarchs War and the Final Battle

19.content: The Rulers are an ancient race of eight angelic warriors created when the Absolute Being split the light at the dawn of time, born to protect the world from their counterparts, the Monarchs, and bound to them in an endless war. After eons of stalemate, the Absolute Being refused to let the Rulers end the war, so seven of the eight Rulers rebelled and killed him; only Ashborn remained loyal, was defeated and left for dead, and survived by transforming into the Shadow Monarch. The Rulers used the Cup of Reincarnation to repeatedly rewind time in failed attempts to prevent the destruction of the human world, and granted fragments of their power to seven human vessels. The Rulers originally tasked their vessels with killing the newly chosen Shadow Monarch, Sung Jinwoo, but later allied with him against the Monarchs after he killed one of them. Several vessels died during the Monarchs War defending the human world. After the Rulers helped end the Monarchs War, they used the Cup of Reincarnation one final time at Sung Jinwoo's request, exhausting its power forever, and later thanked him for ending the war alone.
19.name: Rulers

20.content: Shadow Extraction is a resurrection skill unique to the Shadow Monarch that lets its user raise a slain magic beast or hunter as a loyal shadow soldier by speaking a command word. It costs no mana and can be used on hundreds of corpses at once across a wide radius. At skill level two it gains Shape Transformation, letting its user freely alter the appearance of any shadow soldier created. Each target can only be attempted three times, and success depends on the target's former strength and how long ago it died. Shadow Extraction fails on mana-based beings like Rulers and Monarchs, on contaminated targets like demons, and on targets too strong or too long dead. Sung Suho later bypassed the demon restriction using Antares' power, and Sung Jinwoo bypassed the no-mana restriction after gaining the Shadow Monarch's true power.
20.name: Shadow Extraction

21.content: Shadows are a race of undead soldiers raised from the corpses of dead humans or monsters, stored within a Shadow Monarch's own shadow as loyal servants. Ashborn first commanded the Shadow Army, and Sung Jinwoo inherited both the title of Shadow Monarch and Ashborn's surviving forces. Shadows possess regenerative bodies that cannot be permanently destroyed by ordinary attacks, require no food or rest, and grow stronger by killing monsters in battle, sometimes surpassing the strength they held in life. They are ranked by grade, from weak Normal Grade foot soldiers up through Elite, Knight, Elite Knight, General, Marshal, and finally Grand Marshal, the strongest shadow and the Shadow Monarch's lieutenant. Only General grade or higher can speak, and only in monster tongue. All shadows remain fanatically loyal to their Shadow Monarch regardless of who they were before death and can sense the Monarch's emotions across great distances.
21.name: Shadows

22.content: Sung Jinwoo is a Korean hunter and the founder and guild master of the Ahjin Guild. Sung Jinwoo began his career as an infamously weak E-Rank hunter who risked his life on raids to support his sister, Sung Jinah. Sung Jinwoo is a tall, muscular man with gray eyes and black hair, and his eyes glow purple while using his powers. After nearly dying in the Double Dungeon beneath Cartenon Temple, Sung Jinwoo was chosen by Ashborn as his vessel and successor, becoming the Player of the System and gaining an unmatched growth rate that let him rise to become the strongest hunter on Earth. Sung Jinwoo commands an army of shadow soldiers, including Igris and Beru, can open gates between worlds, and wields umbrakinesis and telekinesis through Ruler's Authority. Sung Jinwoo is devoted to his family and to Cha Hae-In, whom he eventually marries, but is merciless toward anyone who threatens them. After Ashborn transferred his full power to him, Sung Jinwoo defeated the Monarchs, becoming the second Shadow Monarch and one of the strongest beings in existence, and used the Cup of Reincarnation to create a new timeline where he could live a peaceful life with Cha Hae-In and their son, Sung Suho.
22.name: Sung Jinwoo

23.content: Sung Suho is the son of Sung Jinwoo and Cha Hae-In and the main protagonist of the era following his father's generation. Sung Suho is a tall, muscular young man with gray eyes and black hair inherited from his father, softer facial features from his mother, and eyes that glow purple when he uses his powers. Sung Suho is assertive, has a strong sense of justice, and unlike his father is a team player who forms alliances and treats the other future Monarchs as friends. As a child, Sung Suho had most of his shadow monarch powers sealed and his memories of his parents erased, leaving him insecure and bullied at school, though he retained a fondness for ants from his secret childhood friendship with Beru. Sung Suho's drive to grow stronger comes from his wish to reunite with his vanished parents, and his abilities include immense strength and durability, fire magic such as Breath of Destruction, shadow extraction, and Ruler's Authority. Sung Suho is in line to succeed Antares as the next Monarch of Destruction, and Sung Jinwoo struck a bargain with Antares to help train Suho toward that role.
23.name: Sung Suho

24.content: The System is a magical program that appears only to Sung Jinwoo, naming him its sole Player after his first descent into the Double Dungeon beneath Seoul. The System works like a video game, issuing Sung Jinwoo quests, an unlimited inventory, and stats in Strength, Agility, Stamina, Intelligence, and Sense. Each level-up raises all of Sung Jinwoo's stats by one and grants five points to distribute, plus three more for finishing his daily quest. The System also grants new skills and warns Sung Jinwoo of nearby murderous intent. Only Sung Jinwoo can see or use it, and his inventory items cannot be taken without his consent. Kandiaru originally built the System to grow a human vessel strong enough to absorb the Shadow Monarch Ashborn's full power. Once Ashborn transferred his powers to Sung Jinwoo, the System was automatically deleted, though Sung Jinwoo later built a modified version for his son Sung Suho.
24.name: The System

25.content: The Absolute Being was a god of the Itarim race who created the Monarchs and the Rulers at the dawn of time by splitting darkness and light. He treated his creations as entertainment, refusing to end their endless war even when the Rulers begged for the strength to finish it. Seven of the eight Rulers rebelled and killed the Absolute Being on his throne; only Ashborn stayed loyal and was defeated trying to stop them. The Absolute Being could create life and artifacts like the Cup of Reincarnation from nothing, but had no ability to fight, so the Rulers killed him easily.
25.name: Absolute Being

26.content: The Ant King was the hidden boss of the Jeju Island S-Rank Gate, a large humanoid ant with black exoskeleton, glowing red eyes, and sharp claws, with human-level intelligence and arrogance. During the fourth Jeju Island raid, the Ant King killed over eight of sixteen S-Rank Hunters, including Goto Ryuji, and devoured Min Byung-Gyu's head for healing magic and language skills. The Ant King fought Sung Jinwoo with poison and speed without landing a hit, then tried to flee as Jinwoo's shadow army slaughtered his brethren. Jinwoo killed the Ant King and revived him as a shadow soldier renamed Beru.
26.name: Ant King

27.content: The ants were a race of human-sized mutant insects that overran Jeju Island and turned it into a wasteland roughly four years before Sung Jinwoo's awakening, defeating the Korean S-Rank Hunters sent to retake the island. Left undisturbed, the ants bred for generations, eventually evolving the ability to fly long distances once their food supply ran low. The Ant Queen was the last survivor of the first generation. A typical ant soldier could fight an A-Rank Hunter to a standstill. The Korean Hunters Association ultimately wiped out the entire colony during the Jeju Island Raid.
27.name: Ants

28.content: Baek Yoonho is a Korean S-Rank Hunter and Guild Master of the White Tiger Guild, founded after he left the Fiend Guild with several members. Baek is ambitious and protective of his guild, and was the first to realize Sung Jinwoo's strength had no ceiling. Though mediocre in raw power among S-Ranks, he compensates with a Beast-type transformation into a tiger-like humanoid with boosted strength and speed, plus Eyes of the Beast, which gauge an opponent's power. In the revised timeline, Baek became a firefighter and trusted Sung Suho as the grandson of Sung Il-Hwan, whom Baek respects.
28.name: Baek Yoonho

29.content: Baran was the King of Demons and the Monarch of White Flames, one of nine Monarchs allied with the Shadow Monarch Ashborn against the Rulers in the ancient war. A cruel, brash demon, Baran turned on Ashborn at Antares's command, joining Rakan in an ambush after Ashborn slaughtered enemy soldiers. Ashborn defeated both; Rakan fled, but injured Baran mocked Ashborn instead of explaining his betrayal, so Ashborn crushed his skull and absorbed his corpse. His abilities included strength, a demon army, lightning breath, and shapeshifting. Jinwoo later destroyed Baran's clone in the Demon Castle.
29.name: Baran

30.content: Bellion is a servant of Ashborn and the strongest shadow soldier in existence, holding the rank of Grand Marshal in Sung Jinwoo's shadow army. Bellion is a large, winged shadow warrior with jagged black armor and blank white eyes, wielding a centipede-like blade as whip and sword. Born from the fruit of the World Tree, Bellion was Ashborn's first and most loyal lieutenant, transferring that loyalty to Jinwoo after concluding the two Shadow Monarchs share the same character. Bellion arrived in Seoul with Ashborn's shadow army and easily defeated Beru in a sparring match, proving his rank.
30.name: Bellion

31.content: Choi Jong-In is a Korean S-Rank Hunter and Guild Master of the Hunters Guild, recognized as the strongest mage-type hunter in Korea and a rough rival to Baek Yoonho. Choi is sly, friendly, and a habitual chain smoker. His fire magic, including Flame Spear, Flame Prison, Flame Dragon, and Flame Arrow, can level a building with a single spell, backed by a mana pool surpassed by few besides Sung Jinwoo. Choi is vulnerable in melee and nearly died fighting the Ant King during the Jeju Island Raid. In the revised timeline, Choi was recruited early by Woo Jinchul into the new Hunters Association.
31.name: Choi Jong-In

32.content: Demon Castle is an S-Rank instant dungeon created by Kandiaru, inhabited by demons, and shaped like a destroyed, burning version of Seoul. It has one hundred floors with four major bosses: Cerberus on the first, Vulcan on the fiftieth, Metus on the seventy-fifth, and Baran on the hundredth. Ashborn revealed the ruined appearance reflects a real timeline where the Rulers-Monarchs war devastated humanity. After Sung Jinwoo cleared all one hundred floors, Demon Castle reverted to its original, undamaged state.
32.name: Demon Castle

33.content: Goto Ryuji was the strongest hunter in Japan and Guild Master of the Draw Sword Guild. Outwardly calm and polite, Goto was actually arrogant and indifferent to human life, masterminding a plan to lure Korea's S-Rank Hunters to their deaths during the 4th Jeju Island Raid so his guild could abandon the island. Before the raid, Goto sparred Jinwoo with intent to kill, managing only a shallow cut before Jinwoo caught his hand. During the raid, the Ant King ambushed Goto's group, decapitating his subordinate Ishida and then Goto himself, whose pride kept him from recognizing he was outmatched.
33.name: Goto Ryuji

34.content: High orcs are red-skinned orcs and the strongest known orc type, each powerful enough to fight an A-Rank Hunter to a standstill. High orcs first appeared in large numbers inside the Hunters Guild Gate, overwhelming the Hunters Guild's strike team before Sung Jinwoo intervened. High orcs can speak and follow commands, and some served under Kargalgan, an S-Rank high orc who used gravity magic and mind control to dominate the gate. Jinwoo's shadow army defeated the high orcs and killed Kargalgan.
34.name: High Orcs

35.content: Sung Jinwoo traveled to America to represent Korea at the International Guild Conference, a summit of the world's major hunter guilds. During the conference, a sadistic American S-Rank hunter kidnapped Yoo Jinho to interrogate him about his brother's death, prompting Sung Jinwoo to kill the hunter and raise him as a shadow soldier. Sung Jinwoo also fought and defeated Thomas Andre and the Scavenger Guild. Separately, Sung Jinwoo attempted to summon the dragon Kamish as a shadow; the summon succeeded briefly but soon collapsed since too much time had passed since Kamish's death.
35.name: International Guild Conference Arc

36.content: The Japan Crisis Arc began when an S-Rank Gate opened in Tokyo and the international strike squad of sixteen S-Rank hunters sent to clear it proved too weak to contain the threat inside, with several hunters dying. Sung Jinwoo traveled to Japan and entered the gate himself, fought and killed an army of Giants, and confronted the ancient dragon Legia. Sung Jinwoo accepted Legia's Pledge of Trust before killing him, and through that pledge learned of the ancient war between the Monarchs and the Rulers for the first time. During this arc Sung Jinwoo reached level 122.
36.name: Japan Crisis Arc

37.content: The Job Change Arc began when the System sent Sung Jinwoo into another instant dungeon, where he faced an army of knights, assassins, magicians, and archers guarding an empty throne. Sung Jinwoo defeated the army and its commander, the Blood-Red Commander Igris. Completing the dungeon granted him the Hidden Class Necromancer, which immediately promoted into the rarer Shadow Monarch class, along with the title The One Who Overcame Adversity. This gave Sung Jinwoo the ability Shadow Extraction, letting him convert defeated enemies into shadow soldiers, first used on Igris.
37.name: Job Change Arc

38.content: Kaisel is the shadow of Kaisellin, a wyvern that the Monarch Baran used as his battle mount, extracted and renamed by Sung Jinwoo after he killed Baran in the Demon Castle. Kaisel is a large black-scaled wyvern with glowing purple wings, horns, and underbelly. Jinwoo used Kaisel to deliver the Elixir of Life to his mother Park Kyung-Hye, to clear a path through the Ants during the Jeju Island raid, and to save Sung Jinah from the orc Groctar. During the Monarchs War, Kaisel saved Lennart Niermann by biting down on Rakan, letting Jinwoo use Shadow Exchange to take his place.
38.name: Kaisel

39.content: Kamish was an enormous red dragon with five gray horns and glowing yellow eyes who served as the boss of the first S-Rank Gate ever to open, on the west coast of the United States. After emerging, Kamish slaughtered hundreds of S-Rank Hunters and devastated the region before the last five survivors killed him, earning the title of humanity's greatest calamity. His preserved corpse was later revived by Sung Jinwoo as a shadow soldier; freed of the Rulers' control, Kamish bowed to Jinwoo and revealed he had once served the King of Dragons before his body crumbled away.
39.name: Kamish

40.content: Kamish's Wrath is a pair of daggers forged from the fang of the S-Rank dragon Kamish, won by Thomas Andre in their fight and later gifted to Sung Jinwoo as thanks for sparing his life in America. The daggers grant an attack power boost of 1500, second only to Antares' Fangs, are mana-sensitive, and let Jinwoo kill several lesser Monarchs. After the Cup of Reincarnation reset the timeline, Jinwoo set the daggers aside, and decades later the System awarded the worn Kamish's Wrath to his son Sung Suho, who had them restored to full power by dwarves from his shadow army.
40.name: Kamish's Wrath

41.content: Lennart Niermann is the strongest hunter in Germany, ranking twelfth among S-Rank Hunters worldwide, with eyes that glow yellow when he uses his Charge ability to fire blinding energy blasts from gauntlet-like constructs. Lennart Niermann is polite, patient, and selfless, traveling to Korea to seek Sung Jinwoo's protection after Jinwoo's warning about the Monarchs. When Rakan rampaged through Seoul, Lennart attacked him with Charge after Thomas Andre was overwhelmed, was nearly killed until Kaisel and Jinwoo intervened, and later helped Thomas escape.
41.name: Lennart Niermann

42.content: Liu Zhigang is the strongest hunter in China and one of the five National Level Hunters, ranked second behind Thomas Andre. Zhigang is brash and blunt, openly admitting Sung Jinwoo was worlds above him. He fights with a Chinese dao and jian, capable of slicing a giant apart in one Energy Slash Attack, and as a vessel of a Ruler wields Ruler's Authority. In the revised timeline, Zhigang regained his powers decades later and resisted the Itarim's possession. In India, he attacked Sung Suho mistaking him for a criminal, but Suho freed Zhigang using Spring Water, revealing Suho as Jinwoo's son.
42.name: Liu Zhigang

43.content: Magic beasts, also called the Denizens of Chaos, are the magical monsters that inhabit dungeons found beyond Gates, the portals connecting Earth to other dimensions. If a dungeon is not cleared before its time limit expires, a Dungeon Break occurs and its magic beasts escape into the human world, where high-rank specimens devastate cities. Magic beasts span many types, including beasts, demons, dragons, giants, humanoids, insects, and undead. Each carries an Essence Stone inside its body, a resource guilds recover from corpses. The Rulers manipulate most magic beasts to be hostile toward humans.
43.name: Magic Beasts

44.content: National Level Hunters are hunters whose individual strength rivals an entire nation's military. The title began after five S-Rank hunters killed the dragon Kamish and were each granted authority equal to a nation's own; clearing one S-Rank gate is the minimum requirement to join. They are considered the strongest hunters alive and answer to no one. The four strongest, Thomas Andre, Liu Zhigang, Christopher Reed, and Siddharth Bachchan, were secretly empowered by the Rulers for a decade. Sung Jinwoo was never inducted, since the second Ruler-Monarch war began before the world recognized him.
44.name: National Level Hunters

45.content: Querehsha is the Queen of Insects and the Monarch of Plagues, one of the nine Monarchs, able to transform into an insectile horror through Spiritual Body Manifestation. She is bloodthirsty and treats her minions, including Beru, with a possessive, motherly attachment. Querehsha can secrete lethal venom, reanimate corpses, regenerate from grievous wounds, and open gates between worlds. She helped kill Christopher Reed, then joined the Monarchs War against Sung Jinwoo, where she was sliced in half while trying to reclaim Beru and was dissolved by Jinwoo's Mutilation after her venom failed.
45.name: Querehsha

46.content: Rakan is the King of Beasts and the Monarch of Fangs, one of the nine Monarchs, able to transform into a gigantic white wolf through Spiritual Body Manifestation. He is aggressive and contemptuous of humans, but a cowardly hypocrite who flees stronger foes while calling others cowards, and he cares for his heir Gray. Rakan helped kill Christopher Reed, then joined the war on Sung Jinwoo, rampaging through Seoul and fatally stabbing Jinwoo before Jinwoo's resurrection turned the battle. Rakan fled, was hunted down, and was killed in four strikes after begging for his life.
46.name: Rakan

47.content: After the Double Dungeon left him comatose, Sung Jinwoo woke up three days later in a hospital, having survived only because the System had granted him the Player ability. Sung Jinwoo discovered the System functioned like a video game, issuing quests and rewarding completed ones with stat increases and levels. During his recovery the System forced Sung Jinwoo into a deadly trial area filled with poison-toothed giant desert centipedes, which he barely survived. The trial earned him a Dungeon Key, letting him enter a private instant dungeon and beginning his rapid rise in power.
47.name: Reawakening Arc

48.content: A Red Gate is a rare gate type that seals shut once hunters enter, trapping them until the boss dies, the party dies, or a dungeon break occurs. Red Gates look like ordinary blue gates until entered, with harsh environments where one day passes per hour outside, and are deadly without an A-Rank hunter present. Sung Jinwoo was trapped in a Red Gate during a White Tiger Guild exercise where Kim Chul's poor leadership got most of the party killed; Jinwoo killed Kim Chul after Kim Chul went berserk. Later, another Red Gate on a Seoul highway, Jinwoo cleared alone with ease.
48.name: Red Gate

49.content: The Red Gate Arc began when Sung Jinwoo joined a White Tiger Guild training raid alongside Ahn Sangmin and Han Song-Yi. The raid party became trapped inside a rare dungeon whose entrance vanishes until cleared or everyone inside dies, a frozen wasteland of Ice Bears and Ice Elves. Sung Jinwoo's shadow soldiers killed an Ice Bear Alpha's pack, and Igris beheaded the Alpha after it drained Sung Jinwoo's mana. Baruka the Ice Elf and his army arrived and challenged the humans, and Sung Jinwoo instantly killed Baruka's strongest warrior, exposing the group's hidden strength to Korea's guilds.
49.name: Red Gate Arc

50.content: Ruler's Authority, also called Capture, is a telekinetic skill exclusive to the Rulers and their human vessels. At full strength it works even against Monarch-level opponents, costs no mana, and affects a large area, including using its recoil to propel its user through the air. Sung Jinwoo first gained a weaker version called Ruler's Hands from a rune stone taken from Igris; it evolved into full Ruler's Authority during his battle with Kandiaru's statues in the Cartenon Temple. Five vessels are known to have used it: Ashborn, Sung Jinwoo, Sung Suho, Thomas Andre, and Kandiaru.
50.name: Ruler's Authority

51.content: South Korea, formally the Republic of Korea, occupies the southern half of the Korean peninsula and is the homeland of Sung Jinwoo, the strongest hunter in the world. South Korea originally had ten S-Rank Hunters, but only six remain after the other four were killed in action. Foreign nations regarded Korea's hunting community as weak, partly because Korea was the only country to lose territory to a gate when Jeju Island was overrun, until it was later reclaimed. Korean hunter organizations include the Korean Hunters Association and guilds such as the Ahjin Guild and White Tiger Guild.
51.name: South Korea

52.content: Sung Jinah is the younger sister of Sung Jinwoo and a civilian student living in Seoul. After their father Sung Il-Hwan disappeared in a dungeon and their mother Park Kyung-Hye fell into a coma from Eternal Slumber, Jinah was raised largely by Jinwoo, who became the family's sole breadwinner. Jinah is cheerful and diligent, earning top marks in school, but worries constantly about Jinwoo's safety as a low-ranked hunter and has threatened to drop out to support them both. Jinah remains unaware of the System that transformed her brother.
52.name: Sung Jinah

53.content: Tarnak is the King of Monstrous Humanoids and the Monarch of Iron Body, one of the nine Monarchs, whose Spiritual Body Manifestation turns him into a golem-like giant with flaming green hair. Tarnak is egotistical and contemptuous of humanity, dismissing Sung Jinwoo as unfit to be Shadow Monarch for being human. His Iron Body Reinforcement drastically boosts his combat power and nearly immunizes his soul to life-draining effects. Tarnak helped kill Christopher Reed, then was ambushed and killed by Thomas Andre, Bellion, and Beru after Tusk severed his link to Antares.
53.name: Tarnak

54.content: Thomas Andre is the strongest hunter in America, ranked first among the five National Level Hunters, and owner of the Scavenger Guild. Raised in poverty as a brawler, Thomas is proud and aggressive but gracious in defeat. He fought Sung Jinwoo nearly to death at the International Guild Conference, then gave Jinwoo the dagger pair Kamish's Wrath for sparing his guild. During the Monarchs War, Thomas fought Rakan to a standstill before Jinwoo saved him. After the timeline reset, Thomas became a wrestler, regained his powers, and later killed Christopher Reed to become the Monarch of Conquest.
54.name: Thomas Andre

55.content: The White Tiger Guild is one of the top five guilds in Korea, active in the Seoul Metropolitan Area and led by Guild Master Baek Yoonho, an S-Rank fighter, with Baek Miho serving as Vice-Guild Master. The White Tiger Guild has a distinctive training practice of sending new recruits on raids into low-rank gates alongside the guild's stronger members, reserving entire gates for internships under the protection of B-Rank and A-Rank guardians. Notable members include the tanker Kim Chul, who died in battle. Baek Yoonho maintains a friendly rivalry with Choi Jong-In of the Hunters Guild.
55.name: White Tiger Guild

56.content: Woo Jinchul is a Korean A-Rank Hunter who served as Chief Inspector of the Hunters Association's Surveillance Team under Chairman Go Gunhee, succeeding him as Chairman after Gunhee's murder. Jinchul is stoic and fact-focused, but deeply respected Gunhee. He was nearly killed alongside Choi Jong-In and Cha Hae-In in the reappeared Double Dungeon before Jinwoo saved them. In the revised timeline, Jinchul retained his memories first and rebuilt the Hunters Association from nothing for Jinwoo's return; he later began dying from overusing Rune Stone skills, cured by Jinwoo with Xavier's Soulstone.
56.name: Woo Jinchul

57.content: The World Tree is an immense tree rooted in the Sea of the Afterlife, its trunk vast enough to stretch through the void between dimensions. The Absolute Being planted it to balance the war between the Rulers and the Monarchs, using its fruit to produce Ruler soldiers. After the Rulers killed the Absolute Being, the World Tree stopped bearing fruit and is now considered dead. Candidates in a Monarch succession ritual are teleported to its trunk and must defeat Nidhogg, the giant serpent guarding it.
57.name: World Tree

58.content: Yoo Jinho is a Korean D-Rank Hunter, son of Yoojin Construction CEO Yoo Myunghan, and founder and Vice-Guild Master of the Ahjin Guild under Sung Jinwoo. Jinho is naive but fiercely loyal, having left his father's business to back Jinwoo's guild. He met Jinwoo on a strike team led by corrupt hunter Hwang Dongsuk, who tried to have them killed; Jinwoo slaughtered the traitors and dungeon boss, and Jinho came to see him as an older brother. As a weak D-Rank, Jinho funds the guild with expensive equipment. After the Great Cataclysm, Jinho became CEO of Ahjin Soft and raised his nephew Sung Suho.
58.name: Yoo Jinho

59.content: Yoo Soohyun is a famous Korean actress and model, the cousin of Yoo Jinho, and an A-Rank Hunter belonging to the Ahjin Guild. Yoo Soohyun has long brown hair and brown eyes, later dyed pale purple, and dresses casually in public with hats or masks to avoid recognition. Soohyun is affable and charming but has little patience for egotistical people. Sung Jinwoo recruited Soohyun into the Ahjin Guild as its third member because she had no real interest in hunting, and her viral selfie with Jinwoo drew reporters to the guild's first raid.
59.name: Yoo Soohyun

60.content: Baruka's Dagger is a small bright silver blade originally wielded by the Demon Castle guardian Baruka. After Sung Jinwoo killed Baruka, Jinwoo took the dagger as a drop item, using its attack boost of 110 and agility boost of 10 against the Demon King Baran before replacing it with Baran's own Demon King's Daggers.
60.name: Baruka's Dagger

61.content: Cerberus was an A-Rank magic beast that served as the first boss and gatekeeper of the Demon Castle, taking the form of a huge black three-headed dog with glowing red eyes. Cerberus could double his strength for short bursts using Rage and overpowered Sung Jinwoo in their first encounter, before Jinwoo killed him.
61.name: Cerberus

62.content: The Chaos World is a dimension ruled by the Monarchs and inhabited by the Magic Beasts that form their armies, a barren expanse of reddish-brown plains under purple skies. The Monarch Antares states that every Chaos World creature emerging through gates is a remnant of an army the Monarchs lost to the Rulers.
62.name: Chaos World

63.content: Christopher Reed was one of America's two strongest hunters and one of the five National Level Hunters, secretly a vessel of one of the Rulers. Warned by Norma Selner of his death, Reed fought three Monarchs alone at his mansion and was killed, his mutilated body found in the ruins.
63.name: Christopher Reed

64.content: The Cup of Reincarnation is an ornamental chalice created by the Absolute Being and seized by the Rulers after killing him, holding finite power to rewind time by ten years. After defeating Antares, Sung Jinwoo had the Rulers spend its last charge to revive everyone he lost, erasing all memory of himself.
64.name: Cup of Reincarnation

65.content: Demons are vicious magic beasts whose society is stratified into noble clans that grow stronger by consuming souls, an ability that became the basis for the leveling System used by Hunters. Demons are immune to Shadow Extraction. Sung Jinwoo wiped out most demon clans, sparing only the Radiru Clan.
65.name: Demons

66.content: Dragons are winged, fire-breathing magic beasts ranking among the strongest on Earth, capable of the Dragon's Fear, a mana-infused roar that plunges weaker beings into despair. Sung Jinwoo hunted dragons to near-extinction, sparing a single egg; the dragon hatched from it, Ragnar, is Kamish's son.
66.name: Dragons

67.content: The Draw Sword Guild, also called the Blade Guild, was the strongest guild in Japan, originally with eleven S-Rank hunters led by Goto Ryuji. During the fourth Jeju Island Raid, the Ant King killed Goto Ryuji and six other S-Rank hunters, ending the guild's dominance. Reiji Sugimoto became Guild Master afterward.
67.name: Draw Sword Guild

68.content: The Hunters Guild Gate was a top-tier A-Rank gate, the first A-Rank gate Sung Jinwoo cleared alone. Inside, High Orcs led by the gravity-magic boss Kargalgan trapped the guild's strike team until Jinwoo summoned his Shadow Army and defeated Kargalgan, later extracting him as Shadow Soldier Tusk.
68.name: Hunters Guild Gate

69.content: Hwang Dongsoo was a Korean-American S-Rank Hunter of the Scavenger Guild who blamed his brother Hwang Dongsuk's death on Sung Jinwoo and Yoo Jinho. Seeking revenge, Dongsoo kidnapped Jinho but was easily killed by Jinwoo, who extracted his shadow and named it Greed.
69.name: Hwang Dongsoo

70.content: The Hyena Guild is a small Korean guild of former gangsters operating out of Gwanak Mountain, with an average member rank of only D-Rank. The Hyena Guild kidnaps people for the Itarim on its own turf, but Sung Suho discovered the kidnappings and defeated the entire Hyena Guild by himself with ease.
70.name: Hyena Guild

71.content: Itarim's Stone Tablet is a small device created by the Apostles letting hidden Apostles communicate and teleport between linked Gates. Sung Suho and Beru found one in a warehouse of the Tyrant King of Madblood. The Last Paradise's Bank leaders and Yuri used such tablets to direct Gates and Towers at rival territories.
71.name: Itarim's Stone Tablet

72.content: Kasaka's Venom Fang is a small black dagger dropped by the Blue Venom-Fanged Kasaka after Sung Jinwoo killed it. It grants an attack power boost of 25 and carries Kasaka's venom, inflicting Paralyze and a draining Bleed. Jinwoo used it as his main weapon until he bought the dagger Knight Killer.
72.name: Kasaka's Venom Fang

73.content: Kim Chul was an arrogant A-Rank Hunter of the White Tiger Guild who abandoned weaker hunters during the Red Gate Incident, after which his squad was slaughtered by Yetis. Driven insane, Kim Chul attacked Sung Jinwoo and was killed by the shadow soldier Igris, becoming the shadow Iron.
73.name: Kim Chul

74.content: The Knights Guild is one of the top five guilds in Korea, based in the Yeongnam Region and led by Guild Master Park Jongsoo, an A-Rank tanker. The Knights Guild has no S-Rank hunters but boasts the highest number of A-Rank hunters of any guild in the country.
74.name: Knights Guild

75.content: Mana is a natural energy radiating from magical beings and objects, including hunters, magic beasts, and Gates, though it is not native to the human world. Exposure to mana flowing through Gates causes some people to awaken as hunters with magical abilities. A hunter's Hunter Rank is measured from their mana output.
75.name: Mana

76.content: Min Byung-Gyu was a Korean S-Rank Healer and close friend of Baek Yoonho who rejoined for the 4th Jeju Island Raid and was devoured by the Ant King while healing the wounded. Sung Jinwoo briefly revived Min Byung-Gyu as a shadow to heal the dying Cha Hae-In, then released his soul at Yoonho's request.
76.name: Min Byung-Gyu

77.content: The Outer Universes are countless universes created by the Itarim, the Outer Gods, separated by Dimensional Gaps crossable only by force or transfer. A universe whose god dies draws other Outer Gods and collapses without a master. The Outer God War killed several Itarim, and survivors fought over leaderless universes.
77.name: Outer Universes

78.content: Park Heejin is a Korean B-Rank Hunter of the White Tiger Guild and one of five survivors of the Red Gate Incident. Heejin chose to stay with Sung Jinwoo's group rather than join Kim Chul's doomed squad, a decision that saved her life when Kim Chul's team was wiped out by Yetis.
78.name: Park Heejin

79.content: The Penalty Zone is a punishment the System inflicts on Sung Jinwoo whenever he fails his daily quest. It is an endless, starless desert with no wind, sun, or moon, yet visible. Sung Jinwoo must survive four hours there while hunted by giant sand centipedes.
79.name: Penalty Zone

80.content: Sung Il-Hwan is Sung Jinwoo's father and one of Korea's first S-Rank Hunters, granted power by the Rulers and tasked with slaying the future Shadow Monarch. Unable to kill his son, Il-Hwan became Jinwoo's protector, held off Rakan and Sillad in the Monarchs War, then dissolved into light and died in Jinwoo's arms.
80.name: Sung Il-Hwan

81.content: Tank, Iron, and Tusk are three of Sung Jinwoo's shadow soldiers, key members of his army during the Monarchs War. Tank is the shadow of an alpha ice bear, a brute-strength brawler. Iron is the shadow of the slain hunter Kim Chul, an axe-wielder. Tusk is the shadow of the High Orc Shaman Kargalgan, a fire-based caster.
81.name: Tank, Iron, and Tusk

82.content: The Tokyo S-Rank Gate spawned in Tokyo after the Jeju Island Raid crippled Japan's S-Rank Hunters, then broke and released giants that killed millions. Sung Jinwoo arrived days later, killed nearly all the giants, and found Legia, King of Giants, chained inside. Jinwoo refused an alliance and decapitated Legia.
82.name: Tokyo S-Rank Gate

83.content: The World of Eternal Slumber, also called the World of Repose, is the dimension where the Shadow Monarch's soldiers reside under an eternal twilight. Sung Jinwoo, the Monarch of the Dead, carries this realm within his shadow. It holds zones including Bellion's training grounds and an Ant Colony where Beru resides.
83.name: World of Eternal Slumber

84.content: Yoo Jinhee is the older sister of Yoo Jinho. When her father Yoo Myung-Han was diagnosed with the fatal disease Eternal Slumber, Jinhee returned to Korea to care for him and was at his bedside the night he was miraculously cured by Sung Jinwoo.
84.name: Yoo Jinhee

85.content: Yuri Orloff was Russia's strongest S-Rank Hunter, a barrier specialist nicknamed the Saviour for sealing dangerous Gates. During the Tokyo Crisis his barrier was shattered by a giant and Orloff was swallowed whole and killed. In the revised timeline he was revived and rose to become Russia's prime minister.
85.name: Yuri Orloff
